articles.title: Who needs VCs when you have friends like these?
articles.description: Ryan welcomes RunPod co-founder and CEO Zhen Lu to discuss circumventing VC money by going straight to your community for funding, how Zhen balances founder intuition with user feedback when the community is the one backing the project, and RunPod’s journey from basement servers to global infrastruc
articles.content: Ryan welcomes RunPod co-founder and CEO Zhen Lu to discuss circumventing VC money by going straight to your community for funding, how Zhen balances founder intuition with user feedback when the community is the one backing the project, and RunPod’s journey from basement servers to global infrastructure partnerships with a software-layer approach and data-first paradigm.

Google is now doing something about that scourge. Picture the scene: you look up something on Google Search and — instead o
articles.content: Websites that act like a super-chatty colleague who just won't shut up and let you go when a conversation should be over are among the most annoying things on the internet. Google is now doing something about that scourge. Picture the scene: you look up something on Google Search and — instead of relying on potentially hallucinating AI Overviews — you click through to an actual website for your information. But, when you try to leave the site by hitting the back button, your browser doesn’t

articles.title: OpenAI buys its second startup in a month
articles.description: OpenAI has acquired Hiro Finance, a startup that offers AI-powered financial planning tools. As first reported by TechCrunch , fiscal terms of the deal, which was announced on Monday, were not disclosed by OpenAI. However, all signs point this to being an acquhire, with Hiro founder Ethan Bloch writ
articles.content: OpenAI has acquired Hiro Finance, a startup that offers AI-powered financial planning tools. As first reported by TechCrunch , fiscal terms of the deal, which was announced on Monday, were not disclosed by OpenAI. However, all signs point this to being an acquhire, with Hiro founder Ethan Bloch writing on LinkedIn that the company's product would stop working on April 20. Users have until May 13 to migrate their data off of Hiro's servers before everything is deleted. It's unclear if OpenAI plan
articles.author: Igor Bonifacic

articles.title: Understanding OpenClaw Memory: How Your Agent Remembers
articles.description: Originally published on Remote OpenClaw . Every time you start a new session with your OpenClaw agent, you might wonder: does it remember what we talked about last time? The answer depends entirely on how you configure memory. Out of the box, each conversation starts fresh. But with the right memory
articles.content: Originally published on Remote OpenClaw . Every time you start a new session with your OpenClaw agent, you might wonder: does it remember what we talked about last time? The answer depends entirely on how you configure memory. Out of the box, each conversation starts fresh. But with the right memory setup, your agent can retain project context, personal preferences, learned patterns, and accumulated knowledge across sessions. This guide explains every type of OpenClaw memory, how each one works,

articles.title: Retro Rewind re-creates the glorious drudgery of working a '90s video store
articles.description: What the nostalgic throwback lacks in complexity it makes up for in repetitive charm.
articles.content: If you were working a retail job at a movie rental store in the early '90s, there's a decent chance you couldn't wait to clock out for the day and escape from the daily grind with a mindless video game. Here in the 2020s, on the other hand, at least one mindless video game is striving to re-create the daily grind of working at a video rental store. Retro Rewind: Video Store Simulator is the latest in a burgeoning field of "work simulators" that have found indie success on Steam . And while the d
articles.author: Kyle Orland

articles.title: How exposed is your code? Find out in minutes—for free
articles.description: The new Code Security Risk Assessment gives you a one-click view of vulnerabilities across your organization, at no cost. The post How exposed is your code? Find out in minutes—for free appeared first on The GitHub Blog .
articles.content: Most security leaders share the same suspicion: there are vulnerabilities in our codebase that we don’t know about. The uncomfortable truth is that most code never gets a thorough security review. Vulnerabilities accumulate quietly in active repositories, across languages and teams, often undetected until something goes wrong. And if you’re relying on manual reviews or narrowly scoped tools, the gaps may be wider than you think. Today, we’re introducing the Code Security Risk A
articles.author: Dorothy Pearce

articles.title: Has Google’s AI watermarking system been reverse-engineered?
articles.description: A software developer claims to have reverse-engineered Google DeepMind's SynthID system, showing how AI watermarks can be stripped from generated images or manually inserted into other works. A claim that, according to Google, isn't true. The developer, going by the username Aloshdenny, has open-sou
articles.content: A software developer claims to have reverse-engineered Google DeepMind's SynthID system, showing how AI watermarks can be stripped from generated images or manually inserted into other works. A claim that, according to Google, isn't true. The developer, going by the username Aloshdenny , has open-sourced their work on GitHub and documented his process, claiming all it required was 200 Gemini-generated images, signal processing, and "way too much free time." A little weed also seemed to help. "No
articles.author: Jess Weatherbed

articles.title: NZXT agrees to let customers keep their rental PCs in class-action settlement
articles.description: NZXT will forgive up to $5,000 in debt for customers of the Flex program.
articles.content: PC hardware company NZXT and its billing partner, Fragile, have agreed to a $3,450,000 settlement in response to a class-action complaint regarding NZXT’s Flex PC rental program. NZXT announced Flex in August 2024, saying that it would charge customers $59 to $169 a month to rent an NZXT gaming desktop (as of this writing, Flex prices are $79 to $279 per month). At the time, NZXT said that the PCs would be “new or like new.” Subscribers had the option to receive an upgraded rental PC every two y
articles.author: Scharon Harding

articles.title: Amazon buys the satellite internet company behind Apple’s SOS system
articles.description: Amazon has today announced it is merging with satellite internet provider Globalstar Inc. to bolster Leo , its Starlink rival. Globalstar isn’t a household name but you do know its work, as it provides Apple’s emergency satellite connectivity for compatible iPhones and Watches. In a statement, Amazo
articles.content: Amazon has today announced it is merging with satellite internet provider Globalstar Inc. to bolster Leo , its Starlink rival. Globalstar isn’t a household name but you do know its work, as it provides Apple’s emergency satellite connectivity for compatible iPhones and Watches. In a statement, Amazon says the deal will grow Leo’s space-based footprint and enable direct-to-device service for its burgeoning satellite network. An interesting wrinkle is Apple owns 20 percent of Globalstar, which it
articles.author: Daniel Cooper

articles.description: Samsung just released its lineup of Micro RGB TVs for 2026, and it includes models that start at 55 inches and go up to 85 inches. These 4K sets feature the company's Micro RGB display technology, which uses thousands of little red, green and blue LEDs to minimize color bleed and enable "expande
articles.content: Samsung just released its lineup of Micro RGB TVs for 2026, and it includes models that start at 55 inches and go up to 85 inches. These 4K sets feature the company's Micro RGB display technology, which uses thousands of little red, green and blue LEDs to minimize color bleed and enable "expanded color with pinpoint accuracy." The display is assisted by a new AI processor that has been specifically designed to calibrate the picture to bring out all of that gorgeous color. The company says th
articles.author: Lawrence Bonk

articles.content: This is today’s edition of The Download , our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Want to understand the current state of AI? Check out these charts. If you’re following AI news, you’re probably getting whiplash. AI is a gold rush. AI is a bubble. AI is taking your job. AI can’t even read a clock. Stanford’s 2026 AI Index—the field’s annual report card—cuts through the noise. The data reveals a technology evolving faster

articles.content: When it comes to the best noise-canceling earbuds, the competition has gotten fierce. Apple, Sony, Samsung, and Google all offer excellent options, though, unsurprisingly, Bose still retains the noise-canceling crown with the latest QuietComfort Ultra Earbuds . Luckily, you can now grab the second-gen earbuds at Amazon and Best Buy for $249 ($50 off), which matches their second-best price to date. Health officials are now warning residents and travelers about the exposure while trying to directly notify passengers who shared flights with the infected person. articles.description: Learn how to create a free website for any repository on GitHub Pages. The post GitHub for Beginners: Getting started with GitHub Pages appeared first on The GitHub Blog .
articles.content: Welcome back to GitHub for Beginners. So far, we’ve discussed GitHub Issues and Projects , GitHub Actions , and covered a bit about security . This time, we’re going to talk about GitHub Pages. Did you know that you have access to a free and secure hosting service on GitHub, readily available for any project? That’s what GitHub Pages is—a way to turn any GitHub repository with a static website into a live site for free. You just need three things: A GitHub account A proje
articles.author: Kedasha Kerr

articles.title: Google shoehorned Rust into Pixel 10 modem to make legacy code safer
articles.description: Cellular modems are complex black boxes of legacy code, but Google is making them safer with Rust.
articles.content: Modern smartphone operating systems have myriad systems in place to improve security, but none of that helps when attackers target the modem. Google's Project Zero team has shown it's possible to get remote code execution on Pixel phone modems over the Internet, which prompted Google to reevaluate how it secures this vital, low-level system. The solution wasn't to rewrite modem software but rather to shoehorn a safer Rust-based component into the Pixel 10 modem. Cellular modems are something of
articles.author: Ryan Whitwam
